rationale: B is the more coherent AND more falsifiable spec. Coherence: WorktreeOps.List() returns ([]WorktreeListEntry, error) (mindspec_executor.go:32-36, bdcli.go:202) — a cleanly-signalled source, error distinguishable from empty. So B is not a new posture: it slots into R1's existing principle 'gate fail-CLOSED on every cleanly-signalled leg', which currently exempts exactly one leg because BranchExists cannot signal. B moves the merge-prevention job onto a leg that CAN, shrinking the exception instead of widening the residual. R1 is already structured as legs-with-postures; the 'in addition to' variant keeps BranchExists as breadth trigger (branchy-but-worktreeless orphans still refused), keeps Facts 1&2 and AC11/AC12 intact (AC12 becomes the sufficiency argument that worktree-enum covers everything FinalizeEpic can merge), and the Non-Goals refutation SIMPLIFIES to 'closed by construction' — no softening, no contortion. Falsifiability: B admits a deterministic RED-on-revert AC needing no real race — seam-force branchExistsFn=false (the transient) while the bead's worktree exists and is non-ancestor; gate must still refuse; revert to probe-only detection turns it RED. A's residual is definitionally un-pinnable — no test can discriminate 'accepted hole'. A also fails the spec-114 precedent it invokes: audited refutation is for can't-fix-in-the-binary residuals, and B's existence makes 'can't-fix' false — a chose-not-to residual sits incoherently beside Non-Goals entries that are structurally out of reach, under a 'could not break it' claim G2 just broke. Note for round 7: gate-List vs FinalizeEpic-List are still two calls; a worktree added between them is concurrent-mutation class (raw-tamper family) and should be bounded honestly if G2 flags it.
rejected: A: the residual is honest-writable but un-pinnable, and invoking the 114 audited-refutation escape is unsound once a small in-binary fix (B) provably exists — 'can't fix' becomes 'chose not to'.
